MobVnums.brief: A collection of constants representing the virtual numbers (VNUMs) for various mobile entities (mobs) in the game.
MobVnums.details: The MobVnums group defines a set of constant integer values corresponding to the unique virtual numbers (VNUMs) assigned to specific mobile entities such as NPCs, monsters, and special characters within the game world. These constants serve as standardized identifiers for entities like vampires, city guards, skeletons, and quest mobs, enabling consistent referencing, spawning, and management across different parts of the codebase. By centralizing these VNUM definitions, the group enhances code clarity, reduces errors from hardcoded values, and simplifies maintenance when adding or modifying mob types.
MobVnums.notes: The constants are organized to reflect different mob types and roles, ensuring easy identification and usage. This organization supports scalable game design by allowing straightforward addition of new mob VNUMs while maintaining clear distinctions between entity categories.
MobVnums.rationale: This group exists to provide a maintainable and unambiguous way to reference specific mob types throughout the game system. By defining these VNUMs centrally, it ensures consistency in spawning, behavior, and interaction logic, which is crucial for reliable game mechanics and easier updates or expansions of the mob roster.
MobVnums.name: MobVnums

MOB_VNUM_FIDO.brief: The constant stores the virtual number (VNUM) identifying the Fido mobile entity in the game.
MOB_VNUM_FIDO.details: MOB_VNUM_FIDO is a compile-time constant that uniquely identifies the Fido mobile NPC within the game's internal database. It is initialized with the value 3090, which corresponds to the Fido entity's VNUM. This constant is used throughout the codebase whenever the Fido mobile needs to be referenced, such as during spawning, loading, or default assignment processes. Its consistent use ensures that the correct entity is identified and managed across various game functions and data handling routines.
MOB_VNUM_FIDO.notes: This constant is typically used as a default or fallback VNUM in functions that load or spawn mobile entities, providing a reliable reference point for the Fido NPC.
MOB_VNUM_FIDO.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable reference to the Fido mobile's VNUM, facilitating consistent identification and reducing the risk of errors when handling this specific entity in game logic and data operations.
MOB_VNUM_FIDO.name: MOB_VNUM_FIDO

usages.save_8cc, void fread_pet(Character *ch,: Serves as a default vnum value when the JSON object lacks a 'Vnum' field or the vnum is invalid.

MOB_VNUM_HELGA.brief: The constant MOB_VNUM_HELGA stores the unique virtual number identifying the 'Helga' mobile entity in the game.
MOB_VNUM_HELGA.details: This constant defines the specific virtual number (VNUM) assigned to the 'Helga' mobile, serving as its unique identifier within the game's database. Initialized with the value 3011, it is used consistently across the codebase for spawning, referencing, and managing the 'Helga' mobile entity. Its usage ensures that all systems interacting with this mobile, such as spawn routines, scripts, and interaction handlers, correctly identify and operate on the same entity.
MOB_VNUM_HELGA.notes: The VNUM is stored as a compile-time constant, which allows for efficient lookups and prevents accidental modifications. It is part of a set of predefined constants representing different mobile entities, facilitating maintainability and clarity in the code.
MOB_VNUM_HELGA.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, unambiguous reference to the 'Helga' mobile's unique identifier, enabling consistent spawning and interaction logic throughout the game system.
MOB_VNUM_HELGA.name: MOB_VNUM_HELGA

MOB_VNUM_CITYGUARD.brief: The constant representing the virtual number (VNUM) assigned to city guard NPCs in the game.
MOB_VNUM_CITYGUARD.details: This constant holds the unique identifier (VNUM) 3060 used to reference city guard mobile entities within the game's codebase. It is used in spawning routines, behavior scripts, and management functions to ensure consistent identification of city guard NPCs across different modules. The value is set at compile time and remains unchanged, facilitating reliable referencing and interactions with city guard entities in various game systems.
MOB_VNUM_CITYGUARD.notes: The VNUM is stored as a compile-time constant, which helps optimize performance and prevents accidental modification. It is part of a broader set of constants defining various NPC types, enabling organized and readable code when handling different mobile entities.
MOB_VNUM_CITYGUARD.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference city guard NPCs by their VNUM, supporting consistent spawning and behavior logic throughout the game system.
MOB_VNUM_CITYGUARD.name: MOB_VNUM_CITYGUARD

MOB_VNUM_VAMPIRE.brief: The constant representing the unique virtual number (VNUM) assigned to vampire mobile entities in the game.
MOB_VNUM_VAMPIRE.details: This constant defines the specific VNUM used to identify vampire NPCs within the game environment. It ensures consistent referencing across various systems such as spawning, scripting, and behavior control. Initialized to 3404, this value corresponds to the vampire entity's entry in the game's database of mobile entities, facilitating reliable identification and manipulation of vampire mobs throughout the codebase.
MOB_VNUM_VAMPIRE.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time constexpr to optimize performance and prevent accidental modification. It is part of the MobVnums namespace, grouping related entity identifiers for clarity and maintainability.
MOB_VNUM_VAMPIRE.rationale: Having a dedicated constant for the vampire VNUM simplifies code maintenance, reduces errors from hardcoded values, and provides a clear, centralized reference point for all vampire-related mob operations.
MOB_VNUM_VAMPIRE.name: MOB_VNUM_VAMPIRE

MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN.brief: Constant representing the virtual number identifier for patrolman NPCs in the game.
MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN.details: MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN is a predefined constant that holds the unique virtual number (VNUM) assigned to patrolman non-player characters (NPCs) within the game environment. Initialized with the value 2106, it serves as a key identifier used throughout the codebase to recognize and differentiate patrolman NPCs from other entities. This allows functions such as targeting, behavior assignment, and interaction handling to reliably identify patrolmen, ensuring consistent game logic and interactions involving these specific NPCs.
MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN.notes: The constant is typically used in conditional checks to prevent certain NPCs from being targeted or attacked, maintaining game balance and NPC roles.
MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to reference patrolman NPCs by their VNUM, supporting game mechanics that depend on identifying specific NPC types for behaviors, interactions, or restrictions.
MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN.name: MOB_VNUM_PATROLMAN

usages.special_8cc, bool spec_troll_member(Char: Used as a constant to identify patrolman NPCs, preventing the function from targeting them.
usages.special_8cc, bool spec_ogre_member(Chara: Used as a constant to identify patrolman NPCs, preventing the ogre from attacking them.

MOB_VNUM_SKELETON.brief: Constant representing the unique identifier for the skeleton mobile entity in the game.
MOB_VNUM_SKELETON.details: MOB_VNUM_SKELETON is a constexpr integer that uniquely identifies the skeleton mobile (mob) within the game environment. Initialized to 2, it is used in functions such as animate_mob and spell_animate_skeleton to specify the skeleton type when summoning or manipulating such entities. This constant provides a reliable reference point for spawning, managing, and differentiating skeleton mobs from other mobile entities throughout the codebase, ensuring consistency in their identification and handling.
MOB_VNUM_SKELETON.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time value, enabling efficient comparisons and usage in performance-critical functions. Its fixed value helps maintain uniformity across different modules that interact with skeleton mobs.
MOB_VNUM_SKELETON.rationale: This constant exists to standardize the identification of skeleton mobs, simplifying code maintenance and reducing errors when summoning or referencing skeleton entities in various game mechanics.
MOB_VNUM_SKELETON.name: MOB_VNUM_SKELETON

usages.magic_8cc, void spell_animate_skeleton(s: Used as a parameter in animate_mob to specify the specific mobile (skeleton) to be created and summoned.
usages.magic_8cc, void animate_mob(Character *c: Compared against 'vnum' to determine if the summoned mob is a skeleton and to assign it to the character's skeleton field.

MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE.brief: The constant representing the unique virtual number (vnum) assigned to zombie mobs in the game.
MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE.details: MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE is a constexpr integer initialized to 3, which uniquely identifies zombie-type mobile entities within the game environment. It is used extensively in functions responsible for spawning, managing, and referencing zombie mobs, such as animate_mob and spell_animate_zombie. This vnum corresponds to the zombie's data entry in the game's database or data files, ensuring consistent and reliable identification across different subsystems involved in creature creation, behavior, and interaction.
MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE.notes: This constant is typically used in comparisons and assignments related to zombie mob creation, facilitating clear and maintainable code by avoiding magic numbers.
MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to refer to zombie mobs by their designated vnum, supporting consistent spawning and management of zombie entities throughout the game logic.
MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE.name: MOB_VNUM_ZOMBIE

usages.magic_8cc, void spell_animate_zombie(ski: Provides the specific identifier for the zombie mob to be created by animate_mob.
usages.magic_8cc, void animate_mob(Character *c: Compared against 'vnum' to determine if the summoned mob is a zombie and to assign it to the character's zombie field.

MOB_VNUM_WRAITH.brief: Constant representing the virtual number (vnum) for the Wraith mobile entity.
MOB_VNUM_WRAITH.details: MOB_VNUM_WRAITH is a predefined constant that holds the unique identifier (vnum) assigned to the Wraith mobile within the game. Initialized to 4, it is used consistently across the codebase to spawn, identify, and manage Wraith entities, ensuring that operations involving this specific mobile are clear and error-free. Its usage is integral in functions that create or check for Wraiths, such as animation or summoning routines, facilitating reliable referencing and interaction with this particular mobile type.
MOB_VNUM_WRAITH.notes: The constant is used as a key reference in functions that spawn or identify Wraiths, promoting code clarity and reducing errors related to hardcoded vnums.
MOB_VNUM_WRAITH.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to refer to the Wraith mobile's vnum, supporting consistent spawning and identification processes throughout the game logic.
MOB_VNUM_WRAITH.name: MOB_VNUM_WRAITH

usages.magic_8cc, void spell_animate_wraith(ski: Passed to animate_mob to specify the specific mobile (mob) to be created, representing the wraith's vnum.
usages.magic_8cc, void animate_mob(Character *c: Compared against 'vnum' to determine if the summoned mob is a wraith and to assign it to the character's wraith field.

MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE.brief: The constant `MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE` uniquely identifies the Gargoyle mobile entity within the game.
MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE.details: This constant holds the virtual number (vnum) assigned to the Gargoyle mobile, serving as its unique identifier in the game's mobile database. Initialized with the value 5, it is used throughout the codebase to spawn, recognize, and manage Gargoyle mobs consistently. Functions such as `animate_mob` leverage this vnum to instantiate Gargoyle entities, ensuring that the correct mobile template is used during gameplay. The vnum acts as a key reference point for various game mechanics involving Gargoyle mobs, facilitating reliable identification and interaction.
MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE.notes: The vnum value is fixed at 5, which corresponds to the Gargoyle's template in the mobile database, and is used as a constant to prevent magic numbers scattered throughout the code.
MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to reference the Gargoyle mobile's template, enabling consistent spawning and recognition of Gargoyle entities across different game systems.
MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE.name: MOB_VNUM_GARGOYLE

usages.magic_8cc, void spell_animate_gargoyle(s: Used as a parameter in animate_mob to specify the specific mobile (mob) template to create, in this case, a gargoyle.
usages.magic_8cc, void animate_mob(Character *c: Compared against 'vnum' to determine if the summoned mob is a gargoyle and to assign it to the character's gargoyle field.

MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE.brief: The constant representing the virtual number identifier for the resurrected zombie mobile entity.
MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE.details: MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE is a compile-time constant that stores the unique virtual number (VNUM) assigned to the zombie prototype used during resurrection spells. It is utilized in functions such as create_mobile() to instantiate a zombie character with predefined attributes. This constant provides a standardized reference to the zombie prototype across the codebase, ensuring consistent creation and behavior of resurrected zombie NPCs.
MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE.notes: Using a dedicated constant for the zombie VNUM helps maintain consistency and simplifies updates if the prototype's VNUM changes. It also improves code readability by clearly indicating the purpose of the VNUM in resurrection-related functions.
MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE.rationale: This constant exists to facilitate the reliable creation of zombie NPCs during resurrection spells, ensuring that the correct prototype is used and that all resurrected zombies share the same baseline attributes and behaviors.
MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE.name: MOB_VNUM_RESZOMBIE

usages.magic_8cc, void spell_resurrect(skill::t: Passed to create_mobile() to generate a zombie prototype for resurrection.

MOB_VNUM_SQUESTMOB.brief: The constant representing the virtual number identifier for the special quest mobile character.
MOB_VNUM_SQUESTMOB.details: This constant defines the specific virtual number (VNUM) used to uniquely identify the quest mobile NPC within the game environment. Initialized to 85, it serves as a key reference point whenever the quest mob needs to be created, located, or managed in the codebase. Its consistent use ensures that functions responsible for spawning or handling quest-related NPCs correctly identify the intended prototype, facilitating reliable quest mechanics and interactions.
MOB_VNUM_SQUESTMOB.notes: The constant is used primarily in functions that generate or manipulate quest NPCs, such as in the 'generate_skillquest_mob' function, to ensure the correct prototype is referenced during quest setup.
MOB_VNUM_SQUESTMOB.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to identify the quest-specific mobile NPC, supporting consistent spawning and management of quest mobs across the codebase.
MOB_VNUM_SQUESTMOB.name: MOB_VNUM_SQUESTMOB

usages.quest_8cc, void generate_skillquest_mob(: Referenced to create a quest mob prototype; if creation fails, logs a bug and exits.
